meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no version control (Beginner Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no version control. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Pricing to Solve No Version Control
content.seo_description: Learn how to implement SaaS pricing while overcoming version control issues. A beginner-level guide using Antigravity and Claude Code for your portfolio.
content.answer_block: Fix your version control by implementing Git-based tracking alongside your SaaS pricing logic. By treating your pricing tiers as code-managed configurations, you ensure auditability and deployment safety. Use Claude Code to automate the integration of Stripe billing logic, effectively turning your pricing strategy into a versioned, professional-grade software asset.
content.intro:
You are staring at a folder full of 'final_v2_new.py' files, a chaotic graveyard of progress where a single wrong keystroke could wipe out hours of work. The absence of version control isn't just a technical oversight; it is the silent killer of student developer ambition, turning every feature update into a high-stakes gamble with your sanity.

But what if you could transform this fragility into a professional asset? By building a SaaS pricing engine, you force yourself to adopt rigorous development habits. We aren't just writing code; we are architecting a system that handles money, requires precision, and demands the very version control practices that separate amateurs from engineers.

steps.title: Initialize Git and Environment
steps.description: Establish a professional foundation by initializing a repository and setting up your environment variables before writing a single line of business logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Set up a Node.js project structure with a clean .gitignore file and a standard folder architecture suitable for a SaaS pricing application.

steps.title: Define Pricing Schema
steps.description: Create a versioned configuration file for your pricing tiers. This allows you to track changes to your SaaS model over time without hard-coding values.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a JSON schema for three SaaS pricing tiers: Hobby, Pro, and Enterprise, including monthly/yearly prices and feature lists.

steps.title: Implement Stripe Integration
steps.description: Connect your pricing logic to a real payment processor. This step introduces real-world complexity that necessitates a stable version history.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Stripe checkout session handler in TypeScript that reads from the pricing_config.json file.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'integrate stripe checkout with pricing_config.json'

steps.title: Versioned Feature Flagging
steps.description: Use version control to manage which features are available based on the user's selected pricing tier, preventing code regressions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a middleware function that checks user subscription status against the pricing tier and toggles feature access accordingly.

steps.title: Commit and Deploy
steps.description: Finalize your development cycle by committing your changes and preparing for a production deployment, ensuring your work is documented and safe.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a commit message for the completed pricing module that follows conventional commit standards.
steps.claude_code_command: git add . && git commit -m 'feat: implement dynamic saas pricing engine'

pro_tips: Treat your pricing tier definitions as immutable assets; never overwrite them, only version them.
Use git hooks to run linting before every commit to ensure your pricing logic remains bug-free.
Always store your Stripe API keys in a .env file that is ignored by Git to prevent security leaks.
Implement a 'rollback' strategy by tagging your releases; if a pricing change breaks the checkout, revert to the previous tag immediately.
Use Claude Code to document your API endpoints in a README, which counts as a 'living' document for your portfolio.
resources: Git Best Practices for Students: https://git-scm.com/doc
content.next_steps: Once you have your pricing engine running, deploy it to Vercel or Netlify. Then, add a user dashboard to display their current subscription status, completing the full SaaS lifecycle.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting your code rot in disorganized folders. Build a system that scales, tracks, and monetizes, proving you have the skills to ship production-ready software. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
